lines.speaker: Farid Morrisane (ores and bars)
lines.text: I would prefer it if you didn't speak to me in such a manner. I'll have you know I'm an accomplished merchant.

lines.speaker: Farid Morrisane (ores and bars)
lines.text: Don't tell me to calm down! And don't call me 'junior'.

lines.speaker: Farid Morrisane (ores and bars)
lines.text: I'll have you know I am Farid Morrisane, son of Ali Morrisane, the world's greatest merchant!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why are you here and not him?

lines.speaker: Farid Morrisane (ores and bars)
lines.text: My dad has given me the responsibility of expanding our business here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you're up to the task? What a grown up boy you are! Mummy and daddy must be very pleased!

lines.speaker: Farid Morrisane (ores and bars)
lines.text: Look, [mate/darling] - I may be young, I may be short, but I'm a respected merchant around here and don't have time to deal with simpletons like you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you show me the prices of ores and bars?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I best go and speak with someone more my height.

lines.speaker: Farid Morrisane (ores and bars)
lines.text: Then I shall not stop you, [mister/miss]. I've too much work to do.

lines.speaker: Farid Morrisane (ores and bars)
lines.text: My father shall be so pleased.

lines.speaker: Farid Morrisane (ores and bars)
lines.text: Hmm. If divide by 20 and take off 50%...

lines.speaker: Farid Morrisane (ores and bars)
lines.text: I can make so much money here!
